title: Writing the Training Loop
concept: Combining forward pass, loss calculation, backward pass, and optimizer steps across multiple epochs.

exercises.prompt: What is the purpose of the backpropagation pass in training a neural network?

options.text: To run the model and make predictions
To calculate gradients for updating weights

exercises.explain: The backpropagation pass calculates the gradients needed to update the weights in a neural network, which helps the model learn.

cards.title: What's a Training Loop?
cards.body: A training loop is how we train a model by repeatedly teaching it with data, adjusting its parameters until it learns to make accurate predictions.

cards.title: Training Loop Defined
cards.body: The training loop combines several steps: the forward pass, loss calculation, the backward pass, and updating weights with an optimizer, repeating this over multiple epochs.

cards.title: Like a Recipe
cards.body: Think of the training loop like baking cookies. You mix ingredients (forward pass), check if they taste good (loss calculation), fix the recipe if needed (backward pass), and bake again (optimizer). You repeat this until the cookies are just right!

cards.title: Training Loop Example
cards.body: 1. Perform a forward pass to get predictions. 2. Calculate the loss to see how far off the predictions are. 3. Use backpropagation to calculate how to change the weights. 4. Update the weights using an optimizer.

cards.title: Myth: More Epochs Always Help
cards.body: It's a myth that more training epochs will always improve the model. Eventually, the model can overfit to the training data, where it's great at memorizing but bad at generalizing to new data.

cards.title: Training Loop Steps
cards.body: Visualize the process of a training loop with steps to understand it better.

exercises.prompt: Which part of the training loop comes after calculating the loss?
exercises.explain: After calculating the loss, we perform the backward pass to calculate gradients, and then we apply the optimizer step to update the weights based on those gradients.

exercises.prompt: The backward pass occurs before the forward pass in the training loop.
exercises.explain: The correct order is to perform the forward pass, calculate the loss, and then execute the backward pass.

exercises.prompt: In the training loop, after calculating the loss, we perform the __________ pass before updating weights.
exercises.sentence: In the training loop, after calculating the loss, we perform the backward pass before updating weights.
exercises.explain: The backward pass is crucial because it allows us to calculate how to adjust the weights to minimize the loss.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following outlines the correct order of steps in a training loop?

options.text: Forward pass, loss calculation, update weights, backward pass
Forward pass, backward pass, loss calculation, update weights
Loss calculation, forward pass, update weights, backward pass

exercises.explain: The correct order is: Forward pass, calculate loss, backward pass, then update weights. This sequence ensures the model learns effectively.

exercises.prompt: What is the correct sequence of operations for one complete training epoch?

options.text: Forward pass → Loss calculation → Backward pass → Optimizer step
Backward pass → Forward pass → Loss calculation → Optimizer step
Optimizer step → Loss calculation → Forward pass → Backward pass
Loss calculation → Optimizer step → Backward pass → Forward pass

exercises.explain: The correct sequence for one complete training epoch is to first make predictions (forward pass), then evaluate how accurate they are (loss calculation), followed by calculating how to adjust the parameters (backward pass), and finally updating the weights with an optimizer step.
